channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 5 time-saving Alexa commands you’re probably not using yet
item.description: Even if you’re a regular Alexa user, there’s a good chance you haven’t discovered some of its most efficient features. Actually, strike that: There’s a good chance you’re only using your Alexa device to set timers and play music.
item.dc:creator: Doug Aamoth, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft Edge Gains Edge over Chrome with New Resource Controls - Smart Feature Alert!
item.description: Microsoft Edge has just gotten a smart feature that many have been waiting for: the ability to control how much RAM the web browser uses, a boon for gamers too. Learn more about this new resource control feature and how it compares to Chrome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: El plan de transformación digital del Gobierno de Navarra: de la gestión de datos a la capacitación
item.description: En el marco de su plan de transformación digital, el Gobierno de Navarra buscaba una solución integral para gestionar sus datos de manera eficiente y segura.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: La contratación de CIO pega el estirón: Cómo conseguir un puesto de alto nivel en tecnología en 2025
item.description: Los primeros resultados de la contratación de líderes de TI en 2025 sugieren un mercado sólido. Para algunas empresas de contratación, el crecimiento del empleo para puestos ejecutivos tecnológicos está en su punto álgido.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use Gemini AI to write (and rewrite) in Google Docs and Gmail
item.description: Whether you’re a professional writer or someone who dreads having to compose documents or emails for your job, the potential of AI assistance for your writing tasks is appealing.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIO hiring on the rise: How to land a top tech exec role in 2025
item.description: Early returns on 2025 hiring for IT leaders suggest a robust market. For some recruitment firms, job growth for tech executive positions is at great heights.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: El gasto en tecnología y desarrollo de Caixabank crece un 8,3% en 2024
item.description: Caixabank sella el 2024 anotando una inversión de 1.368 millones de euros en el apartado destinado a tecnología y desarrollo. Una cifra al alza que evidencia los esfuerzos de transformación digital de la entidad bancaria al registrar un crecimiento del 8,3% interanual.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How CIOs can help the future suck less than the present
item.description: CIOs who want to prosper in the future must escape a less-than-celebrated present. Technology in general has slipped out of favor. Science fiction has been taken over by dystopia and apocalyptic scenarios. Public discourse frequently devolves into argumentation over end times.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple shareholders not expected to scrap diversity programs despite broader backlash
item.description: Apple shareholders are expected to reject an attempt to pressure the technology trendsetter into scrapping corporate programs designed to diversify its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: DNA Testing Risks: What You Need to Know
item.description: DNA testing services have become increasingly popular, but with them come risks to your personal data and privacy. Learn what you need to know before taking a DNA test.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nvidia's RTX 5070 Ti may be getting the competition it needs as the AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT's performance leaks
item.description: It's now or never for AMD with its new GPU lineup amid Nvidia's RTX 5000 series issues, as a new leak reveals the Radeon RX 9070 XT's performance gains over previous GPUs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: US intensifies scrutiny of the EU’s Digital Markets Act
item.description: In a significant escalation of transatlantic tensions over tech regulation, US House Judiciary Chairman Jim Jordan has demanded clarification from the EU antitrust chief Teresa Ribera regarding the enforcement of the EU’s digital marketplace rules, citing concerns about potential discrimination agai
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 10 machine learning mistakes and how to avoid them
item.description: Machine learning technology is taking hold across many sectors as its applications are more widely adopted. The research firm Fortune Business Insights forecasts the global machine-learning market will expand from $26.03 billion in 2023 to $225.91 billion by 2030.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI coding assistants are on a downward spiral
item.description: We’re living in a strange time for software development. On the one hand, AI-driven coding assistants have shaken up a hitherto calcified IDE market.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A smarter approach to training AI models
item.description: Artificial intelligence, which was thought to be handily dominated by American labs and researchers, was thrown for a loop with DeepSeek’s R1. And yet, R1 is still subject to many of the same pitfalls as other models—we must make radical advances to move beyond the current limitations of AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 5 tips for mastering virtual communication
item.description: Andrew Brodsky is a management professor at McCombs School of Business at the University of Texas at Austin. He is also CEO of Ping Group and has received numerous awards, including being chosen by Poets & Quants as one of the Best 40-Under-40 MBA Professors of 2023.
item.dc:creator: Next Big Idea Club, Fast Company

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple drops encryption feature for UK users after government reportedly demanded backdoor access
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple said Friday it will stop offering an advanced data security option for British users after the government reportedly demanded that the company provide backdoor access for any data those users have stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Google’s new AI co-scientist aims to speed up the scientific discovery process
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI co-scientist built on its Gemini 2.0 platform, aiming to accelerate scientific research by generating novel hypotheses and refining experiments. While the system shows promise, questions remain about its performance and broader applicability.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple introduces the iPhone 16e, a fast and powerful mid-range smartphone
item.description: Apple’s new iPhone 16e combines three innovations in one $599 package: an A18 chip, Apple’s fast smartphone processor; Apple Intelligence, the company’s private-by-design take on artificial intelligence; and the C1, Apple’s first-ever 5G radio.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s quantum ambition: One million qubits in the palm of your hand
item.description: Microsoft announced a breakthrough in quantum computing today, unveiling a new kind of quantum processing unit, using a new type of material, to create what it says is a “radically different type of qubit.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Smart home device maker exposes 2.7 billion records in huge data breach
item.description: Major IoT data breach: Mars Hydro database with 2.7 billion records exposed. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the breach is a reminder of the risks that come with using IoT devices.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: More used Seagate HDDs disguised as new: Fraudsters make it tough to spot the difference
item.description: The controversy surrounding used Seagate hard drives has significantly widened, with fraudsters now targeting the company’s IronWolf and IronWolf Pro NAS drives alongside the previously affected Exos server-grade HDDs.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future
item.description: A recent challenge sponsored by NIST tasked drone designers with creating a system to help first responders map indoor environments. The post How drones may be a big help to first responders in the future first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: 7 time-saving Linux commands
item.description: Some Linux commands can make your time on the command line easier and more productive. This post covers some commands that can come in very handy. 1.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World
